title: Kevin Warsh, Trump’s Fed Pick, Has Tough Task Shedding ‘Sock Puppet’ Label
summary: Kevin M. Warsh, at his confirmation hearing to lead the Federal Reserve, sought to dispel concerns that he would cut interest rates to please President Trump.

title: RFK Jr. Says His Department Advises All Children to Get Measles Vaccine
summary: Testifying on Capitol Hill, Robert F. Kennedy Jr. continued to back away from his criticism of the measles shot. But he spoke on behalf of his department, not himself.

title: Iran Says It Seized Two Ships
summary: Also, China quietly builds an island in disputed waters. Here’s the latest at the end of Wednesday.

title: Iran Again Tightens Its Grip on Shipping in the Strait of Hormuz
summary: Traffic in the strait has all but halted as Iran renews its attacks, striking two vessels on Wednesday. More than 300 ships linked to Iran have passed through the strait since the war began.

title: How the War Powers Act Could Pressure Trump to End the Iran War
summary: A decades-old law allows the president to wage war without congressional approval for 60 days, then limits his options for continuing. President Trump may seek to get around it.

title: Not a Deal-Breaker: White House Downplays Iranian Action Near the Strait
summary: President Trump’s threats have given way (for the moment, at least) to a more conciliatory tone about Iran’s actions in the Strait of Hormuz.

title: F.B.I. Said to Have Investigated Times Reporter After Article on Patel’s Girlfriend
summary: The bureau said it is not pursuing a case, but the scrutiny is an example of the Trump administration weighing whether to criminalize routine news gathering.

title: How ‘Yes’ Won a Narrow Victory in Virginia’s Redistricting Battle
summary: Northern Virginia carried the measure to victory even as turnout in Democratic areas lagged and nearly all of the state shifted right from last year’s governor’s race.

title: Kalshi Fines and Suspends 3 Political Candidates for Betting on Their Races
summary: The prediction market said the candidates violated new rules. The platform and its competitors face growing scrutiny from lawmakers and regulators over how political betting is policed.

title: Redistricting Fight Turns to Florida and the Courtroom for Frustrated Republicans
summary: A victory for Democrats in Virginia has left Republicans grumbling about their strategy and looking to the next phase of a coast-to-coast battle.

title: As Democrats Outraise Republican Candidates, the G.O.P. Has This $600 Million Edge
summary: Even as the party faces a worrisome political environment and its candidates are outraised by Democratic ones, powerful Republican groups and super PACs have built a colossal advantage.

title: A Capitol Police Officer, Bogus Accusations and a Jan. 6 Conspiracy Theory
summary: Shauni Kerkhoff was wrongfully implicated in the notorious Capitol Hill pipe-bomb case. Can she ever fully move on?

title: A $440,000 Breast Reduction: How Doctors Cashed In on the No Surprises Act and Arbitration
summary: A law meant to end surprise medical billing accidentally created a multibillion-dollar industry that is making doctors richer.

title: D4vd Murder Case: Celeste Rivas Hernandez’s Cause of Death Is Revealed
summary: The Los Angeles police sought to keep the findings secret for months as they investigated the singer D4vd, who was charged this week with the murder of the teenager.

title: How the Southern Poverty Law Center Drew the Ire of Conservatives
summary: Before the Justice Department filed charges against it, the group had faced scandal and critiques from both the left and the right.

title: ‘Free Births’ Are a New Pregnancy Trend. Critics Warn About Serious Risks.
summary: Promoters of free birthing reject any type of medical intervention during pregnancy or delivery. The movement has been trending on social media, but critics warn it poses serious risks.

title: College Where Charlie Kirk Was Killed Revokes Graduation Speaker’s Invite
summary: Utah Valley University was thrilled that Sharon McMahon, a best-selling author, would speak at its graduation. And then her old posts resurfaced.

title: Aging in Place: How Technology Might Help You Grow Old at Home
summary: The budding field is turning dreams into reality for older adults who are eager to age in place, filling caregiving gaps and easing minds as America ages rapidly.

title: Yale Has Come Up With a Surefire Way to Make a Terrible Situation Worse
summary: Don’t retreat from the world; engage it.

title: ‘The Rich Don’t Play by the Rules. So Why Should I?’
summary: Why petty theft might be the new political protest.

title: Restrictions on Transgender Students Violated Law, New York Finds
summary: Two school districts had similar policies that required students to use facilities that were gender neutral or aligned with their sex assigned at birth.

title: Alan Osmond, Who Led His Brothers in a Boy Band, Dies at 76
summary: He was the eldest original member of the Osmonds, a family singing group that hit it big in the 1970s with songs like “One Bad Apple.”

title: Drake’s Ice Installation for New ‘Iceman’ Album Brings Headaches to Toronto
summary: Fire crews melted a 25-foot-tall ice structure erected to promote the rapper’s forthcoming album, “Iceman,” less than a week after an explosion for a Drake music video shook part of the city.

title: King Charles Will Not Meet Epstein Victims During U.S. State Visit
summary: Representative Ro Khanna had asked Buckingham Palace for a private meeting between the victims and King Charles during his planned visit to the U.S. next week.
